This is an example of a simple **Faded** Parsons Problem. This type of problem has inline fill-in-the-blank inputs that make the problems more difficult to solve (particularly by brute force). The grading for these problems works the same way as for regular Parsons Problems and uses the [standard python autograder](https://docs.prairielearn.com/python-grader/#overview) with a test suite to determine correctness. def fibonacci(n: int): #pin if n <= ___: return 1 return fibonacci(___) + fibonacci(___)
